name: collapse-the-three-parallel-20260408-1934
idea:
Collapse the three parallel resize session kinds in the video-editor ('clip' / 'group-child-boundary' / 'group-edge') into ONE unified ClipEdgeResizeSession with a single applyClipEdgeMove function that dispatches on context internally. This is the missing structural item from the previous cleanup megaplan — that one extracted shared math functions but left the session lifecycle (setup → update → end → commit) triplicated, and the triplication is the cause of 'I can't even tell which path the user's gesture went through' during debugging.

State BEFORE this megaplan:

DONE:
- One ResizeSession discriminated union with three kinds: 'clip', 'group-child-boundary', 'group-edge'
- Shared pure math functions in src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.ts: computeGroupChildBoundaryResize, computeGroupEdgeResize
- cursorOffsetPx captured at pointer-down for all three kinds
- Single ref-backed preview store via useSyncExternalStore
- useTimelineScale hook centralizing pixel↔time
- 12 end-to-end resize integration tests in TimelineCanvas.test.tsx covering off-center cursor on interior boundary and group-edge drags
- 222/222 video-editor tests passing
- tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit clean for video-editor
- A temporary [resize-diag] console.log in the 'group-child-boundary' branch of updateResize (needs removing)

NOT DONE (this megaplan):
- The session lifecycle is still TRIPLICATED: three branches each in handleResizePointerDown, updateResize, endResize
- 'group-edge' uses a special startGroupEdgeResize closure pattern that captures commitResize at session start
- useClipResize.onActionResizeEnd has its own internal branching for 'interior vs free' via getInteriorNeighbor

Target shape:

ONE session type: ClipEdgeResizeSession with fields { pointerId, rowId, clipId, edge: 'left'|'right', cursorOffsetPx, initialBoundaryTime, context: ClipEdgeResizeContext }. The discriminator moves from session to context sub-object.

ClipEdgeResizeContext =
 | { kind: 'free'; initialStart; initialEnd; minStart?; maxEnd? }
 | { kind: 'interior'; pairStart; pairEnd; draggedClipId; adjacentClipId; draggedInitialStart; draggedInitialEnd; adjacentInitialStart; adjacentInitialEnd }
 | { kind: 'outer'; shotId; trackId; groupInitialStart; groupInitialEnd; groupClipIds; groupChildrenSnapshot: Array<{clipId;start;end}> }

ONE core function in lib/resize-math.ts:
applyClipEdgeMove(context, edge, newBoundaryTime) -> { updates: Array<{clipId, start, end}>, wasClamped: boolean }
Dispatches internally on context.kind. 'free' returns one update, 'interior' calls computeGroupChildBoundaryResize, 'outer' calls computeGroupEdgeResize and projects children.

ONE updateResize: computes newBoundaryTime from clientX - cursorOffsetPx, calls applyClipEdgeMove, writes updates to override store. Zero branches on kind.

ONE endResize: one branch on cancelled, no branch on session kind. Calls applyClipEdgeMove one more time and invokes onClipEdgeResizeEnd with updates.

ONE commit path in useClipResize.ts: onClipEdgeResizeEnd takes { session, updates } and builds one applyEdit. startGroupEdgeResize closure is REMOVED.

ONE handleResizePointerDown: uses new resolveClipEdgeResizeContext helper to resolve context up-front based on rows/shotGroups/positionedShotGroups/clipId/rowId/edge, then constructs the unified session.

Hard requirements:
1. All 222 existing video-editor tests pass (some may be rewritten to test by context)
2. The 12 end-to-end resize integration tests survive unchanged
3. Add at least one new end-to-end test for free-clip resize (the 'free' context)
4. tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit clean for src/tools/video-editor/
5. The three session kinds are physically gone: grep for kind: 'group-child-boundary' and kind: 'group-edge' in resize lifecycle code returns zero. Three update branches in updateResize and three end branches in endResize are gone. startGroupEdgeResize closure removed.
6. applyClipEdgeMove exists in lib/resize-math.ts with unit tests covering all three contexts
7. The [resize-diag] console.log is removed
8. No regressions in: free clip resize, interior boundary drag, outer edge drag, body drag inside pinned group (useClipDrag - should be untouched), cross-track grouped drag, switch to/from final video, auto-save deferral, migration load, cursor offset tracking

Constraints:
- Scope: resize lifecycle only
- Touch: TimelineCanvas.tsx, useClipResize.ts, lib/resize-math.ts, lib/resize-math.test.ts, TimelineCanvas.test.tsx, possibly TimelineEditorContext.tsx/useTimelineState.types.ts (if startGroupEdgeResize exposed)
- Do NOT touch useClipDrag.ts, data shape, save/sync/persistence, projection migration
- Use existing computeGroupChildBoundaryResize, computeGroupEdgeResize, computeClipResizeMetaPatch inside the new dispatcher — don't reimplement math
- Prefer Edit over Write. Follow CLAUDE.md
- Leave pre-existing non-video-editor test/tsc issues alone

Success criteria:
1. 'boundary doesn't track cursor during drag' symptom cannot reproduce in unified path
2. tsc clean for src/tools/video-editor/
3. All video-editor tests pass — >=223 after adding free-clip test
4. Grep for kind: 'group-child-boundary' and kind: 'group-edge' returns zero in resize code
5. Grep for startGroupEdgeResize returns zero
6. [resize-diag] console.log gone
7. applyClipEdgeMove and resolveClipEdgeResizeContext exist with unit tests
8. Resize lifecycle code line count DECREASES net
